name: Incheon (South Korea)

colors.symbolism: the sea, the harbour, and Incheon's identity as a port and gateway city
purity, peace, and the bright future of the city
the silvery, brushed tone of the city's modern logotype

description: A blue field charged at the centre with a white stylised emblem incorporating the letters 'INCHEON' and a swirling abstract motif evoking sea waves and the wings of a flying bird, with the silver-grey 'Fly Incheon' city-brand wordmark below.
history: Incheon, a Metropolitan City (Gwangyeoksi) since 1995 and the location of South Korea's principal international airport, adopted its current flag and emblem in their present form in 2014, succeeding earlier designs of 1995 and 2003. The motif combines the abstract letter forms of INCHEON with stylised waves and wings, expressing the city's dual role as a port and as Korea's main aviation gateway.

features.symbolism: the Yellow Sea and Incheon's role as Korea's main maritime and aviation gateway

features.role: Incheon city brand emblem and 'Fly Incheon' wordmark
features.symbolism: stylised letters spelling INCHEON, fused with a swirling motif evoking sea waves and bird wings; together they represent Incheon as a global gateway by sea and air
